agentic-engineering: Use when operating as an agentic engineer on complex tasks. Provides eval-first execution, task decomposition into smaller testable pieces, and cost-aware model routing.
ai-first-engineering: Use when setting up AI-first development workflows for teams. Defines the operating model where AI agents generate implementation output, with review and quality processes.
api-design: Use when designing REST API endpoints, choosing HTTP methods and status codes, implementing pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, or rate limiting.
backend-patterns: Use when building backend APIs, designing database access layers, implementing caching or authentication, or optimizing server-side Node.js, Express, or Next.js API routes.
coding-standards: Use when writing or reviewing TypeScript, JavaScript, React, or Node.js code. Enforces naming conventions, immutability patterns, error handling, async patterns, and function size limits.
content-hash-cache-pattern: Use when caching file processing results to avoid reprocessing unchanged files. Implements SHA-256 content hash caching — path-independent, auto-invalidating, with service layer separation.
continuous-agent-loop: Use when running autonomous agent loops, setting up continuous agent pipelines, or implementing quality gates with evals and recovery controls for long-running tasks.
continuous-learning: Use when extracting reusable patterns from sessions, learning from completed work, or managing instincts. Creates atomic instincts with confidence scoring that evolve into skills, commands, and agents.
cost-aware-llm-pipeline: Use when optimizing LLM API costs, routing tasks to cheaper models, setting up budget tracking, implementing retry logic, or enabling prompt caching for AI pipelines.
database-migrations: Use when creating database migrations, altering schemas, performing data migrations, implementing zero-downtime deployments, or managing rollbacks across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Django, TypeORM).
deployment-patterns: Use when setting up CI/CD pipelines, configuring health checks, implementing rolling or blue-green deployments, creating production readiness checklists, or deploying to Kubernetes.
docker-patterns: Use when writing Dockerfiles, configuring Docker Compose, setting up container networking, managing volumes, or implementing multi-stage builds for secure containers.
e2e-testing: Use when writing Playwright end-to-end tests, setting up Page Object Model, configuring E2E test pipelines, managing test artifacts (screenshots, videos, traces), or fixing flaky tests.
eval-harness: Use when creating evaluation frameworks for agent workflows, defining eval test cases, measuring agent quality and performance, or implementing eval-driven development (EDD).
frontend-patterns: Use when building React components, managing frontend state, optimizing UI performance, implementing lazy loading, error boundaries, or accessible interfaces in React/Next.js apps.
iterative-retrieval: Use when searching for code across a large codebase and initial search is too broad. Progressively refines context retrieval queries to find relevant files, solving the subagent context problem.
plankton-code-quality: Use when setting up auto-formatting and linting on every file edit. Enforces write-time code quality with Plankton -- formatting, linting, and agent-powered fixes via hooks.
postgres-patterns: Use when writing PostgreSQL queries, designing database schemas, creating indexes, implementing Row Level Security, or optimizing query performance with Supabase patterns.
python-patterns: Use when writing Python code. Enforces PEP 8 style, type hints, Pythonic idioms (EAFP, comprehensions, dataclasses), proper exception handling, and async patterns.
python-testing: Use when writing Python tests with pytest, setting up test fixtures, mocking dependencies, parametrizing test cases, or measuring code coverage. Enforces TDD methodology for Python.
regex-vs-llm-structured-text: Use when deciding between regex and LLM for parsing or extracting data from structured text. Decision framework: start with regex, add LLM only for low-confidence edge cases.
search-first: Use when starting a new implementation task. Research existing tools, libraries, and patterns before writing custom code. Search for solutions first, then implement.
security-review: Use this skill when adding authentication, handling user input, working with secrets, creating API endpoints, or implementing payment/sensitive features. Provides comprehensive security checklist and patterns.
strategic-compact: Use when context window is getting large, conversation needs compacting, or you want to preserve important context through task phases. Suggests strategic compaction at logical intervals.
tdd-workflow: Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.
ui-ux-pro-max: Use when generating design systems, choosing UI styles, selecting color palettes and font pairings, or getting stack-specific UI guidelines. Contains 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 fonts across 13 tech stacks with BM25 search.
verification-loop: Use when verifying code changes before committing or pushing. Runs build, tests, lint, and type checks in a comprehensive verification loop to catch errors early.
